mechanism: Potent FLT3 and AXL inhibitor
additional_notes: Alternative name: ASP2215. Approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3‑mutant AML; active against FLT3‑ITD MOLM‑13. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Selective FLT3 inhibitor, potent against FLT3‑ITD
additional_notes: Alternative name: AC220. Strong activity in FLT3‑ITD models (MOLM‑13/MV4‑11). Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Multi‑kinase inhibitor including FLT3
additional_notes: Alternative name: PKC412. Approved in combination for newly diagnosed FLT3‑mutant AML. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Multi‑kinase inhibitor with FLT3 activity
additional_notes: Used off‑label and in trials for FLT3‑mutant AML; active in FLT3‑ITD lines. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Multi‑target TKI, inhibits FLT3 at higher potency
additional_notes: Shown to inhibit some FLT3 mutants in preclinical reports. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Selective BCL‑2 inhibition, induces apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in AML in combination regimens; potent activity in many AML cell lines including MOLM‑13 per literature. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Thioredoxin reductase inhibitor; increases ROS and induces apoptosis
additional_notes: Reported activity against AML cell lines and primary blasts; some activity in FLT3‑ITD models. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Copper‑dependent proteasome and aldehyde dehydrogenase interference; induces ROS (often studied with Cu2+)
additional_notes: Preclinical cytotoxicity in AML when combined with copper. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Mitochondrial uncoupler; inhibits Wnt/STAT3/Notch pathways
additional_notes: Reported antiproliferative activity in AML models including MOLM‑13 in screens. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Multiple effects including mitochondrial/ion channel perturbation and NF‑κB modulation
additional_notes: Identified in screens for AML cytotoxicity. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Disrupts microtubules/tubulin
additional_notes: Shown to inhibit growth of various cancer cell lines including leukemia models in preclinical studies. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG‑CoA reductase; affects prenylation and cell signaling leading to pro‑apoptotic effects
additional_notes: Statins (e.g., simvastatin) have shown pro‑apoptotic effects in AML cell lines and studied in combinations. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Activates AMPK and inhibits mitochondrial complex I
additional_notes: Epidemiologic and preclinical evidence for antiproliferative effects in hematologic malignancies and potential sensitization of AML cells. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Lysosomal/autophagy inhibition
additional_notes: Used in combination strategies to inhibit autophagy and sensitize AML cells. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Inhibits SRC and ABL family kinases (broad‑spectrum TKI)
additional_notes: Has activity in subsets of AML and can synergize with other inhibitors. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: JAK1/2 inhibition (JAK‑STAT pathway)
additional_notes: Relevant where JAK‑STAT pathway is activated; variable AML cell line responses reported. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: MEK1/2 inhibition (MAPK pathway)
additional_notes: Potentially effective in AML with RAS pathway activation. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Off‑target effects on hedgehog, angiogenesis and mTOR pathways
additional_notes: Identified in repurposing screens for anticancer activity; some preclinical hematologic data. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: COX‑2 inhibition and COX‑independent pro‑apoptotic effects
additional_notes: Reported pro‑apoptotic effects in AML cell lines and studied in combinations. Source text did not provide test concentrations.

mechanism: Multiple off‑target effects including dopamine receptor antagonism and mitochondrial effects
additional_notes: Phenothiazines emerged in screens for cancer stem cell and leukemia cytotoxicity. Source text did not provide test concentrations.
